usageNotes.es-ES: conducir is standard formal register; manejar also understood
aparcar is standard; estacionar is also valid formal
usageNotes.es-PR: parquear is standard; from English park
usageNotes.es-CU: parquear is standard; estacionar is formal
usageNotes.es-DO: parquear is standard; estacionar is formal
usageNotes.es-PA: parquear is standard; from English park
usageNotes.es-US: parquear is standard; from English park
usageNotes.es-CR: parquear is common; estacionar also valid
usageNotes.es-BZ: parquear is standard; English is co-official

usageNotes.es-MX: facturar is used in service/commercial billing contexts; cobrar is the general verb for charging

usageNotes.es-PR: congelar is standard; frizar/frisar is informal anglicism
usageNotes.es-US: congelar is standard; frizar is informal anglicism
usageNotes.es-DO: congelar is standard; frisar is informal anglicism

usageNotes.es-ES: charlar is standard; platicar is understood but uncommon
usageNotes.es-MX: platicar is standard for casual conversation

usageNotes.es-ES: echar de menos is standard; extrañar sounds American

usageNotes.es-MX: enojarse is standard across all registers
usageNotes.es-AR: enojarse is standard; enfadarse sounds peninsular
usageNotes.es-CO: calentarse is informal standard; enojarse also understood

meaning: to remain, to stay, to agree to meet
to carry, to wear, to take
to know (a person/place), to be acquainted with
to must, to should, to owe
to wait, to hope, to expect
to leave, to split, to depart
to be worth, to be valid
to gather, to meet, to assemble
